company.name: Marquardt GmbH
company.ownership: Family-owned (3rd-generation Marquardt family; Marquardt Management SE as holding entity)

ceo.name: Björn Twiehaus
ceo.note: Succeeded Dr. Harald Marquardt who led the company for ~29 years (1996–2024)

revenue.note: Down 3.2% vs. 2023 (€1.4B) due to automotive headwinds and slower e-mobility ramp

employees.note: Approximately 9,700 as of 2024 (official site); some sources cite up to 10,200

divisions.name: Human Machine Interfaces (HMI)
divisions.description: Highly integrated user interfaces and intelligent lighting systems for premium vehicles — steering wheel modules, touch lighting, seat switches, rotary controls.

divisions.name: Entry and Authorization Systems
divisions.description: Vehicle access and driver authorization — digital key, ignition start switches, smartphone-based drive authorization, ultra-wideband (UWB) modules.

divisions.name: Power and Energy Solutions
divisions.description: Battery management systems (BMS) for EVs, sensors for hydrogen fuel cells, digital vehicle communication components. New Erfurt plant operational since mid-2024.

divisions.name: Mechatronic Devices
divisions.description: Switches, parking brakes, and drive authorization for trucks and off-highway vehicles. Also covers power tools, household appliances, and heating systems.

divisions.name: Home and Industrial Solutions
divisions.description: Newly created business unit (2024) targeting non-automotive markets — building technology, HVAC, smart home, and industrial sensors including the ultrasonic flow sensor family.

productGroup.name: Sensor Solutions
productGroup.scope: Flow sensors (mechanical + contactless), pressure sensors, temperature sensors, proximity, turbidity, infrared spectrum analysis

families.name: Contactless Flow Sensors
families.status: Current strategic focus — subject of this report

product.name: Ultrasonic Flow Sensor
product.family: Ultrasonic Flow Sensor for Heating Technology
product.homeMarketTitle: Air-Conditioning and Warm Air Heating Equipment Manufacturing

variants.note: Air-water heat pumps — current production. Basis for all market analysis in this report.

q1.german: Welche Anwendungen für diesen Sensor gibt es, wie attraktiv sind diese Markt-Segmente und wie könnte ein passendes Geschäftsmodell aussehen?
q1.english: What applications exist for this sensor, how attractive are these market segments, and what business model would fit?
q1.answer: Eight serious applications were evaluated; five advance to full analysis. The two highest-attractiveness, lowest-effort segments are Data Center Direct Liquid Cooling (Fit 8.50 STRONG) and Fuel Cell Systems (Fit 7.75) — both require firmware and calibration changes only, no new silicon. The scale play is EV Battery Thermal Management on a 36–48 month automotive platform cycle; the defensive anchor is Residential HVAC. The fitting business model is a dual Business Model Canvas scaled by Razor-and-Blade, Ingredient Branding, and Performance-Based Contracting — progressing from sensor → platform → service.

q2.german: Wo führt eine unpräzise / nicht vorhandene Durchflusserkennung oder ein Ausfall des Sensors zu den höchsten Kosten?
q2.english: Where does imprecise or absent flow detection — or a sensor failure — cause the highest costs?
q2.answer: Ranked by leverage ratio (cost-of-failure ÷ sensor price), the highest-cost failure points are Data Center Direct Liquid Cooling (~10³–10⁴×, €1–5M per leak event + €50–200k/hr per AI-rack throttle), EV Battery Thermal (~10³–10⁴×, €500k–5M per thermal runaway), and Fuel Cells (~10²–10³×, €30–80k per silent-stall stack write-off). In every case the dominant failure class is 'missing flow detection' — silent stall, reverse flow, leak signature — exactly what the sensor's bubble/empty-pipe/reverse-flow diagnostic stack catches. Strategic implication: sell on avoided cost, not sensor price.

portfolioPriorities.hardwareDelta: Firmware only (leak-anomaly classifier, OCP pressure qualification)
Firmware + calibration (−40°C, H₂ materials, LIN frames)
Firmware + sub-zero calibration (existing DN20, ASIL B/C)
BACnet + MID today; DN50–100 spool-piece roadmap 2028–2029
portfolioPriorities.role: Roadmap — addressable today, full range gated

financials.note: All figures are directional estimates for scenario framing; Phase 3 Business Model workshop refines with real BOM quotes and Marquardt controlling burden rates before any funding decision.
